subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: 2nd pass (2026-05-28, bias-corrected): non-US read (Al Jazeera/Doha, IMF, Eurostat, Nikkei) confirms the direction. Iran ceasefire is fragile ('on life support'); SCOTUS struck the IEEPA tariffs - a real institutional check - though a Section 122 surcharge was re-imposed; IMF COFER shows dollar erosion is real but slow. Net confirmed.

Live-data refresh (2026-05-28): factors re-validated against diverse, independent sources (multilateral bodies + non-US/non-Anglophone press; state-self-reported data still excluded). Notes mark what moved.

v2.0 source-discipline re-run applied. Post-2022 US official data treated as PARTIALLY-ENTANGLED (documented statistical-agency interference: BLS commissioner fired over a jobs print, ~20-25% agency staffing cuts, USDA food-security survey eliminated). Confidence on official-data-dependent economic factors (inflation, fiscal) lowered; the distortion is assumed to skew official figures positive, so true values likely sit slightly worse than headline.

Trade re-weighted 0.05 -> 0.17 and scored on ACTIONS: the volatile tariff regime manufactures hostile foreign-trade relationships and pushes partners to diversify away from the US - a near- AND long-term cost, not merely a price effect.
Reserve-currency factor kept two-horizon (+5 near / -2 long): a large near-term privilege that becomes a long-term structural dependency as the institutions it shielded are exposed.
Slant-balance audit: negatives are independently sourced (RWB, ASA, OECD, courts); reserve-currency and personal-market strengths credited at full near-term weight to avoid an anti-subject lean.
Scored on observable ACTIONS/DATA over stated intentions; the optional strict-foundational-weighting variant was NOT applied.

economic.notes: Negative across most sub-factors. Reserve-currency privilege provides a large near-term cushion that erodes long-term (resource-curse-like dynamic for the privilege itself). Official-data factors carry lowered confidence under the entanglement discount; trade now weighted 0.17 and scored on tariff-policy actions.

fiscal_state.notes: IMF Article IV (Apr 2026, fully independent): deficit ~7-7.5% GDP, debt >140% by 2031, flagged a global tail risk. Two 2026 shutdowns; debt-ceiling pressure live. Confidence raised - independent/multilateral corroboration now anchors this, not entangled official data.

monetary_fed_independence.notes: Apr 2026 FOMC held rates (4 dissents); Warsh in as chair but seen as weak; SCOTUS signaled it will likely BLOCK the Cook removal (rule-of-law positive). Independence stressed but courts are protecting governors - long-term nudged up.

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: USD reserve share 56.9% (Q3 2025, IMF COFER) - a 31-yr low; central banks net-sold USD and gold now exceeds Treasuries as primary reserve asset. Near-term privilege intact (+5) but long-term erosion accelerating - long nudged to -3.

inflation.notes: April 2026 CPI +3.8% y/y (highest since 2023), independently corroborated (PIIE, Cleveland Fed); ~40% of the gain from the Iran oil shock, tariffs adding ~0.8% to core PCE. Direction now confirmed by independent estimates, not the entangled CPI alone.

banking_stability.notes: Large banks capitalized; regional CRE exposure; the reserve-status backstop itself is eroding.

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Scored on ACTIONS, not just tariff levels: a volatile, unpredictable tariff regime manufactures hostile foreign-trade relationships and pushes long-standing trading partners to diversify away from US supply and demand - a structural long-term cost on top of the near-term shock. Industrial capacity is middling and slow to reshore. Not resource-cursed, but the self-inflicted policy volatility is the trade story.

institutional.notes: Most clearly negative category; multiple independent sub-factors point consistently down. The statistical-integrity finding is the documented basis for the economic-category entanglement discount. Long-term composite degrades further.

rule_of_law.notes: Courts mostly functioning and complied with, but pressure on judges, DOJ action against opponents, selective defiance. Eroding from a strong baseline.

statistical_integrity.notes: BLS commissioner fired over a jobs number; 20-25% agency staffing cuts; USDA food-security survey eliminated; proposed consolidation under political control. ASA: 'deepening crisis.' Independently documented - this is what triggers the entanglement discount on official economic data.

civil_service_capacity.notes: Schedule F reclassification, RIFs, loss of technical expertise, politicization of appointments.

press_freedom.notes: RWB ranking down; legal/FCC pressure on outlets; access restrictions. Diversity remains; no formal censorship.

political_social.notes: Driven heavily by treatment of non-citizens (most relevant to relocation). Long-term composite worsens as demographics deteriorate under immigration restriction.

political_stability.notes: Power transferred in 2024, but 2020 aftermath, election-official pressure, and extreme polarization. High uncertainty re: 2028.

civil_liberties.notes: Surveillance expanding, 1A/4A pressure, large state-level variation.

treatment_non_citizens.notes: ICE detention peaked ~70.8k (Jan); ~540k deported; ~70% of detainees without convictions; $100k H-1B fee; SCOTUS barred court review of visa revocations; birthright EO still blocked, ruling due ~late June. Edging worse; well-documented (TRAC, American Immigration Council).

social_cohesion.notes: Rising political violence and threats; historic-low social trust; large-scale violence not materialized.

demographics.notes: Positive growth via immigration now being restricted; below-replacement native fertility; aging.

alliance_reliability.notes: NATO strain, Five Eyes degraded, EU strategic autonomy, Greenland/Canada friction, transactional posture replacing values-based alliances.

conflict_involvement.notes: Non-US sources (Al Jazeera) read the US-Iran ceasefire as more fragile than US framing - 'on life support,' both sides issuing conflicting reports (late May 2026). De-escalated from active war but unstable; held at -5 with downside risk. Taiwan tension and partner-directed rhetoric persist.

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Sanctions weaponization is expanding alternatives; capital-control risk low but non-zero; expatriate exposure rising.

physical_practical.notes: Healthcare drives most of this. Long-term composite worsens as climate compounds.

climate.notes: SW water stress, Gulf/Atlantic hurricanes, Western wildfire; insurance-market failures FL/CA; federal response capacity under stress.

healthcare.notes: ~2x peer per-capita spend, worse aggregate outcomes; coverage gaps; public-health infrastructure degraded; vaccine policy politicized.

infrastructure.notes: Aging but functional; IIJA deploying at reduced pace; uneven grid; underfunded water in places.

crime_safety.notes: Violent crime down from the 2020-21 peak but firearm violence high by peer standards; highest incarceration rate among wealthy nations.

disaster_insurance.notes: FL/CA insurance markets in crisis; wildfire/hurricane coverage unavailable in high-risk areas; federal flood program underfunded.

summary: Institutional erosion from a high baseline - pressure on the courts, documented statistical-agency interference, civil-service politicization - combined with reserve-currency erosion, alliance decay, and harsh treatment of non-citizens drive a strongly negative living read. Post-2022 official data is discounted as partially-entangled (confidence on inflation and fiscal lowered), and trade is scored on the hostility its volatile tariff actions create rather than on tariff levels alone, which pushes the economic read worse. The reserve-currency privilege is a large near-term cushion that erodes over the long term as the institutions it shielded are exposed.
